sense.definition: a power tool used for smoothing and refining surfaces by abrasion, typically through the use of sandpaper or abrasive discs, helping to remove material or achieve a desired finish

examples.x: He used a sander to smooth the rough edges of the wooden table.
The carpenter quickly finished the project by using an orbital sander on the door frame.
She applied a fine-grit sandpaper to the sander for a smooth finish on the surface.
The mechanic used a sander to remove rust from the metal part before painting it.
The old furniture looked much better after being polished with a sander.

sense.definition: a power tool equipped with a continuous loop of sandpaper or abrasive belt that rotates on two rollers

examples.x: He used the belt sander to smooth out the rough edges of the wooden table.
The carpenter relied on the belt sander to quickly strip the old paint from the door.
She carefully guided the belt sander across the floor, removing the imperfections.
For large sanding jobs, a belt sander is much more efficient than a hand sander.
After finishing with the hand tools, he used a belt sander to speed up the process.

sense.definition: a power tool that combines both rotary and orbital motion to provide a random sanding pattern

examples.x: The carpenter used a random orbital sander to smooth the edges of the wooden table.
To achieve a flawless finish, he switched to a random orbital sander after the initial sanding.
She grabbed the random orbital sander to finish polishing the wooden floor.
After sanding with the random orbital sander, the cabinet surface looked perfectly smooth.
Using the random orbital sander, he was able to quickly remove the old paint from the door.

sense.definition: a power tool used for sanding and shaping curved surfaces, featuring a rotating spindle with sanding sleeves

examples.x: For the detailed work on the trim, a spindle sander provided the precision needed.
He turned to the spindle sander to quickly finish the irregular shape of the shelf.
When refinishing the antique chair, she used a spindle sander for the delicate details.
After cutting the shape, he used a spindle sander to ensure the surface was even and smooth.

sense.definition: a metal tool with a rough surface that is used to smooth wooden or metal rough edges

examples.x: She used a metal file to sharpen the blades of her gardening shears.
The sculptor employed a fine-grit file to refine the contours of the clay sculpture.
He used a diamond file to precisely shape the metal components of the jewelry.
The mechanic used a half-round file to deburr the edges of the metal part.

sense.definition: a coarse woodworking tool with a series of individually cut teeth or ridges, used for shaping and smoothing wood or other materials by abrasion
a type of paper that has sand or another rough material stuck to one side, used for making surfaces smoother or shinier

a small handheld woodworking tool with a cutting blade set at a low angle to the sole of the plane, primarily used for shaping and smoothing wood surfaces, chamfering edges, and removing thin layers of material

examples.x: The woodworker used a block plane to smooth the edges of the tabletop.
To achieve a clean finish, he grabbed the block plane to trim the excess wood.
After cutting the boards, she ran the edges through the block plane for a smoother feel.
He carefully adjusted the blade of the block plane before starting the fine sanding work.
She reached for the block plane to level the surface before applying the final coat of varnish.

sense.definition: a handheld tool with a replaceable blade that features a perforated metal surface for shaping and smoothing wood or other materials

examples.x: When shaping the curved piece of wood, he grabbed the surform plane for faster material removal.
The carpenter used a surform plane to quickly smooth the rough edges of the wooden board.
She preferred the surform plane over a traditional rasp because it worked more efficiently on the softwood.
After using the surform plane, the wood felt much smoother and ready for finishing.
He reached for the surform plane to shape the curved edge of the chair leg more precisely.

sense.definition: a woodworking hand tool with a curved blade set in a handle, designed for shaping and smoothing concave or convex surfaces of wood

examples.x: The carpenter used a spokeshave to smooth the curved edge of the chair leg.
He carefully shaped the wooden handle with a spokeshave to fit his grip.
After the rough cut, she used a spokeshave to refine the shape of the wooden bowl.
The woodworker reached for his spokeshave to finish the curve on the wooden toy.
She used the spokeshave to remove small amounts of wood and perfect the round edges of the chair.

sense.definition: a woodworking tool that removes thin layers of material from a workpiece, resulting in a smooth and consistent surface

examples.x: He used a planer to smooth out the rough edges of the wooden boards.
The carpenter ran the large plank through the planer to achieve a consistent thickness.
She grabbed the hand planer to fix the uneven surface on the piece of wood.
The old workbench had a built-in planer for shaping wood easily during construction.
The woodworker set the planer to a finer setting for detailed smoothing work on the cabinet.

sense.definition: a wheel-shaped abrasive tool used for sharpening, shaping, or smoothing surfaces by removing material through the frictional action of abrasive grains bonded to the wheel

examples.x: The mechanic used a grinding wheel to sharpen the tools before starting the repair work.
The stone carver relied on a grinding wheel to shape the marble into detailed sculptures.
He adjusted the grinding wheel to ensure a perfect edge on the knife blade.
The technician mounted a new grinding wheel onto the machine to continue working on the metalwork.

sense.definition: a machine tool that rotates a workpiece so one can shape it by cutting, sanding, or drilling

examples.x: The machinist used a lathe to shape the metal rod into a precise cylindrical form.
A CNC lathe can automatically cut, drill, and shape materials according to programmed specifications.
The lathe operator carefully adjusted the cutting tools to achieve the desired dimensions and surface finish.
Modern lathes are equipped with digital readouts and computer controls for increased accuracy and efficiency.
In the workshop, the lathe is an essential tool for manufacturing components such as shafts, pulleys, and bushings.

sense.definition: a power tool or machine with a rotating abrasive wheel or disc used for cutting, grinding, or polishing various materials
a woodworking machine used to create a flat and smooth surface on the face or edge of a board

examples.x: After cutting the rough lumber, the woodworker ran it through the jointer to create flat surfaces.
The carpenter used a jointer to smooth the edges of the wooden boards before joining them together.
Before gluing the planks together, the craftsman made sure to run them through the jointer.
Using a jointer helped make sure that all the edges of the wood were straight and even.

sense.definition: a hand tool used in woodworking to flatten and straighten the edges and surfaces of boards

examples.x: The carpenter used a jointer plane to smooth the rough edges of the board.
After cutting the wood, he ran it through the jointer plane to ensure the surface was perfectly flat.
She grabbed the jointer plane to straighten the edge of the wood before joining it with another piece.
With the jointer plane, he carefully smoothed the surface, making sure it was even for the next step in the project.

sense.definition: a handheld power tool with a spinning abrasive disc for cutting, grinding, and shaping materials

examples.x: He used an angle grinder to cut through the metal pipe quickly.
The workers employed an angle grinder to smooth out the rough edges of the concrete slab.
She grabbed the angle grinder to remove the rust from the car’s body.
He wore safety goggles while operating the angle grinder to protect his eyes from flying debris.
After sharpening the blades, he used an angle grinder to polish the metal surface.

sense.definition: a woodworking hand tool used for finely smoothing and leveling wood surfaces to achieve a smooth and polished finish

examples.x: The carpenter used a smoothing plane to remove the rough texture from the wooden board.
After shaping the wood with a rough plane, he finished it with a smoothing plane for a smooth surface.
She carefully guided the smoothing plane along the wood to create a flawless finish.
He reached for the smoothing plane to refine the edges of the table after sanding it.
The woodworker applied the smoothing plane to eliminate any imperfections before applying varnish.

sense.definition: a hand tool used in masonry work to create consistent and smooth joints between bricks

examples.x: The mason used a brick jointer to smooth the mortar joints and give the wall a professional finish.
After laying the bricks, he used the brick jointer to create a concave joint, ensuring water would run off easily.
To give the brick wall a clean look, she carefully ran the brick jointer along the mortar lines.
The mason selected a larger brick jointer for the thicker mortar joints in the exterior wall.

sense.definition: a woodworking tool consisting of a flat base and a cutting blade, used for smoothing, shaping, and leveling wood surfaces by removing thin layers of material

examples.x: The carpenter used a hand plane to smooth the rough edges of the wooden table.
After sanding, he finished the surface with a hand plane for a finer, more polished look.
She grabbed the hand plane to level the wooden boards before assembling the frame.
He used a hand plane to remove the excess wood and make the surface even and flat.

sense.definition: a flat-bladed tool with a handle used for applying and smoothing drywall joint compound during the taping and finishing process

examples.x: The contractor used a taping knife to smooth out the joint compound on the drywall seams.
She carefully applied the joint compound with the taping knife to fill in any gaps between the sheets of drywall.
After the compound dried, he used a taping knife to smooth the surface and remove excess material.
To ensure the drywall seams were perfectly smooth, he used a taping knife with a wide blade.
The drywall installer handed me the taping knife so I could help with the finishing touches.

sense.definition: a hand tool used in woodworking for smoothing and refining the surface of wood

examples.x: The carpenter used a cabinet scraper to smooth the rough edges of the wooden cabinet doors.
To achieve a flawless finish on the tabletop, he carefully ran the cabinet scraper over the surface.
She preferred using a cabinet scraper over sandpaper for its ability to remove imperfections without leaving scratches.
After sanding, the woodworker used a cabinet scraper to refine the finish and ensure it was perfectly smooth.
The skilled artisan used a cabinet scraper to create a clean, polished look on the intricate wooden details.

sense.definition: a hand tool with a sharp metal blade and a handle used for cutting, shaping, and carving wood by striking or pushing it with a mallet or by hand pressure

examples.x: The carpenter used a wood chisel to carefully carve the intricate patterns on the wooden panel.
He picked up a wood chisel and began shaping the edges of the chair legs.
After sanding, the wood chisel helped refine the fine details of the sculpture.
She held the wood chisel steady as she tapped it with a mallet to shape the wooden beam.
Using a wood chisel, the craftsman smoothed out the rough edges of the door frame.

sense.definition: a metal tool with a handle and a strong flat-edged blade that is used to shape hard objects, such as wood, metal, etc.

examples.x: He used a chisel to carve intricate designs into the wood.
The stonemason relied on a chisel to shape the marble blocks.
She learned how to use a chisel safely in her woodworking class.
The chisel's sharp edge made it effective for detailed work.
The set included different sizes of chisels for various tasks.

sense.definition: a specialized woodworking tool with a square or hexagonal body and a beveled cutting edge, used for cutting or cleaning out square corners or recesses in woodwork

examples.x: The carpenter used a corner chisel to clean up the sharp edges of the wooden frame.
He carefully applied the corner chisel to ensure the joints were perfectly square.
To create precise, clean corners, she used a corner chisel on the cabinet's edges.
The builder reached for the corner chisel when it was time to finish the mortar joints.
After cutting the wood to size, the carpenter used a corner chisel to refine the inside corners.

sense.definition: a woodworking tool used for cutting rectangular slots or mortises in wood

examples.x: The carpenter used a mortising chisel to create the perfect slots for the wooden joints.
To assemble the wooden frame, the builder carefully cut the mortises with a mortising chisel.
He struck the mortising chisel with a mallet to make precise cuts in the oak wood.
The mortising chisel made it easier to carve out the deep, clean holes for the door hinges.
She reached for the mortising chisel when the drill was too large for the delicate work.

sense.definition: a small, narrow-bladed hand tool used in masonry and brickwork for applying and shaping mortar, particularly in the process of pointing or filling the gaps between bricks or stones to create a neat and finished appearance

examples.x: The mason used a pointing trowel to carefully fill the gaps between the bricks.
After laying the bricks, he smoothed the mortar with a pointing trowel for a clean finish.
She picked up the pointing trowel to work on the finer details of the stone wall.
The small size of the pointing trowel made it easier to reach the tight spaces in the wall.
He used the pointing trowel to refine the mortar joints, ensuring they were straight and even.

sense.definition: a specialized tool used to create a flared end on a pipe or tubing, allowing for secure connections with other fittings or components

examples.x: He used a flaring tool to widen the pipe ends before attaching the fittings.
The plumber grabbed the flaring tool to ensure a secure connection between the pipes.
The mechanic used a flaring tool to make sure the brake lines were properly connected.
For the heating and cooling system installation, they used a flaring tool to seal the connections.

sense.definition: a versatile handheld power tool that utilizes oscillating motion to perform a variety of tasks such as cutting, sanding, scraping, and grinding

examples.x: I used an oscillating multi-tool to remove the grout between the tiles.
For my home renovation project, I found that the oscillating multi-tool was essential for precise cuts.
I quickly switched attachments on the oscillating multi-tool to grind down the metal surface.
The oscillating multi-tool was perfect for cutting through the wood in tight corners.

sense.definition: a heavy-duty mechanical tool used in metalworking that delivers powerful and repetitive blows to shape or forge metal

examples.x: The blacksmith used a power hammer to shape the hot metal into a horseshoe.
In the metal shop, a power hammer was used to flatten the steel into thin sheets.
The factory relied on a power hammer to produce large quantities of metal parts quickly.
The craftsman carefully adjusted the settings on the power hammer before starting the forging process.
He could feel the vibrations from the power hammer as it struck the iron, molding it into shape.

sense.definition: a tool used for shaping or forming materials, typically in metalworking or machining processes
